content.text: Datasheets for Datasets (Gebru et al., 2021) are a documentation standard for ML training datasets analogous to the component datasheets used in electronics manufacturing. Just as a capacitor datasheet tells an engineer the operating voltage, tolerance, and failure modes, a dataset datasheet tells an ML practitioner what the data contains, how it was collected, what it is appropriate for, and what risks it carries.

The framework organises documentation into seven sections of structured questions. These questions are not all the content — they are the starting points. Answering them forces dataset creators to surface assumptions, gaps, and ethical considerations that would otherwise remain implicit.

attrs.alt: Datasheet sections: Motivation, Composition, Collection, Preprocessing, Uses, Distribution + Maintenance — each with specific sub-questions

content.text: Why does this dataset exist? Who created it and with what resources? Understanding motivation reveals potential conflicts of interest and frames appropriate use:

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class DatasheetMotivation:
    purpose: str                # What task(s) was the dataset created for?
    creators: list[str]         # Individual/team names
    funding_sources: list[str]  # Who paid for creation?
    creation_date: str
    contact_email: str
    known_limitations_of_purpose: str  # Scope the purpose honestly

# Example
motivation = DatasheetMotivation(
    purpose=(
        "A fine-tuning dataset for customer support LLMs. "
        "Intended to improve ticket triage accuracy for SaaS billing inquiries."
    ),
    creators=["AcmeCorp AI Team", "DataOps contractor: VendorX Inc."],
    funding_sources=["AcmeCorp internal budget Q4 2024"],
    creation_date="2024-11-01",
    contact_email="ai-data@acmecorp.example",
    known_limitations_of_purpose=(
        "Dataset is scoped to billing domain only. "
        "Not suitable for general customer support or other domains."
    ),
)

What does the dataset actually contain? The composition section must answer concretely — raw counts, distributions, formats, and any confidential or sensitive content. Vague answers like "various text samples" are not acceptable:

from dataclasses import dataclass, field

@dataclass
class DatasheetComposition:
    instance_count: int
    instance_type: str           # "text", "image", "text-pair", etc.
    format: str                  # "JSONL", "CSV", "Parquet", etc.
    total_size_bytes: int
    label_distribution: dict[str, int]  # label -> count
    missing_data_fields: list[str]      # fields with missing values
    missing_data_fraction: dict[str, float]  # field -> fraction missing
    contains_pii: bool
    pii_types_present: list[str]        # e.g. ["name", "email", "ip_address"]
    pii_handling: str                   # how PII was addressed
    contains_sensitive_categories: bool
    sensitive_categories: list[str]     # per GDPR Art. 9
    is_confidential: bool
    confidentiality_notes: str
    relationships_to_other_datasets: list[str]

composition = DatasheetComposition(
    instance_count=42_000,
    instance_type="text-pair (user_message, agent_response)",
    format="JSONL",
    total_size_bytes=128_000_000,
    label_distribution={
        "billing_dispute": 12_600,
        "payment_method": 9_240,
        "subscription_change": 8_400,
        "technical_issue": 6_720,
        "refund_request": 5_040,
    },
    missing_data_fields=["resolution_time", "agent_id"],
    missing_data_fraction={"resolution_time": 0.18, "agent_id": 0.04},
    contains_pii=True,
    pii_types_present=["name", "email", "billing_address"],
    pii_handling="HIPAA Safe Harbor de-identification applied; PII replaced with [TYPE] tokens",
    contains_sensitive_categories=False,
    sensitive_categories=[],
    is_confidential=True,
    confidentiality_notes="Internal use only; do not distribute externally",
    relationships_to_other_datasets=["Derived from AcmeCorp CRM export v3.2"],
)
How was the data obtained? This section is the most ethically loaded — it determines whether individuals whose data is included gave informed consent and whether collection was lawful:

@dataclass
class DatasheetCollection:
    collection_mechanism: str         # "direct observation", "API scrape", "crowdsource", "existing dataset"
    collection_software: list[str]    # tools and versions used
    time_frame_start: str
    time_frame_end: str
    collectors: str                   # individuals or teams
    validated_by: str                 # who checked for quality/appropriateness
    consent_obtained: bool
    consent_mechanism: str            # how was consent obtained
    notification_provided: bool       # were subjects informed of collection
    notification_mechanism: str
    personal_data_collected_directly: bool  # from individuals themselves
    third_party_sources: list[str]          # any external data sources
    third_party_licenses: list[str]
    ethical_review: bool
    ethical_review_details: str

collection = DatasheetCollection(
    collection_mechanism="Export from internal CRM ticketing system",
    collection_software=["Zendesk API v2", "python-zendesk 2.0.1", "pandas 2.1"],
    time_frame_start="2023-01-01",
    time_frame_end="2024-09-30",
    collectors="AcmeCorp DataOps team",
    validated_by="AI team lead + 2 senior support agents (10% random sample review)",
    consent_obtained=True,
    consent_mechanism=(
        "Customers consented via Terms of Service (Section 8.2: data may be used "
        "to improve our services). Updated privacy policy notified by email 2023-03-01."
    ),
    notification_provided=True,
    notification_mechanism="Privacy policy update email; in-app banner",
    personal_data_collected_directly=True,
    third_party_sources=[],
    third_party_licenses=[],
    ethical_review=True,
    ethical_review_details="Internal AI Ethics Review Board, case #2024-17, approved 2024-10-05",
)

@dataclass
class DatasheetPreprocessing:
    raw_data_preserved: bool          # is original raw data retained?
    raw_data_location: str
    cleaning_steps: list[str]         # ordered list of transformations
    labelling_method: str             # "human", "crowdsource", "programmatic", "model-generated"
    labeller_count: int
    labeller_instructions: str        # or reference to annotation guide
    labeller_demographics: str        # describe if relevant to bias
    inter_rater_agreement: dict[str, float]  # metric -> value
    quality_thresholds: dict[str, float]
    software_used: list[str]

preprocessing = DatasheetPreprocessing(
    raw_data_preserved=True,
    raw_data_location="s3://acmecorp-data-lake/crm-export/raw/ (access-controlled)",
    cleaning_steps=[
        "Removed tickets < 10 words or > 2000 words",
        "Dropped tickets with agent_response = '[BLANK]'",
        "HIPAA Safe Harbor PII de-identification (python-presidio 2.2)",
        "Removed 847 tickets flagged by content safety classifier (threshold 0.8)",
        "Language detection; kept English-only (langdetect 1.0.9)",
        "Deduplicated on normalised user_message (Jaccard similarity > 0.95 → drop)",
    ],
    labelling_method="Existing agent responses used as labels (no re-labelling)",
    labeller_count=0,  # labels are production agent responses
    labeller_instructions="N/A — production responses used directly",
    labeller_demographics="N/A",
    inter_rater_agreement={},
    quality_thresholds={"min_agent_satisfaction_score": 3.5},  # only tickets where CSAT >= 3.5
    software_used=["python-presidio 2.2", "langdetect 1.0.9", "pandas 2.1", "datasketch 1.5"],
)
This is the section most likely to prevent harm if written honestly. Specify not just intended uses but which uses are explicitly prohibited and which may cause unintended harm:

@dataclass
class DatasheetUses:
    intended_tasks: list[str]
    tasks_tested_for: list[str]       # tasks specifically validated
    out_of_distribution_warnings: list[str]
    prohibited_uses: list[str]
    impact_on_people: str
    tasks_to_avoid: list[str]         # uses that could cause harm even if not intended

uses = DatasheetUses(
    intended_tasks=[
        "Fine-tuning LLMs for customer support ticket triage",
        "Training intent classifiers for billing-related queries",
        "Evaluating support response quality",
    ],
    tasks_tested_for=[
        "Ticket intent classification (F1 = 0.89 on hold-out set)",
        "Response quality ranking (human eval, n=500)",
    ],
    out_of_distribution_warnings=[
        "Data is US-market only; may not generalise to EU/APAC billing practices",
        "Covers 2023-2024 only; subscription pricing changes since then not reflected",
        "English-only; multilingual performance unknown",
    ],
    prohibited_uses=[
        "Training models for any use outside AcmeCorp (contractual restriction)",
        "Releasing any subset externally — dataset is confidential",
        "Training models that make autonomous decisions without human review",
        "Re-identification or deanonymisation attempts",
    ],
    impact_on_people=(
        "Dataset contains de-identified data from AcmeCorp customers. "
        "Misuse could expose billing dispute patterns or support interaction styles. "
        "Models trained on this data could inherit agent response biases."
    ),
    tasks_to_avoid=[
        "Profiling individual customers",
        "Training models for credit decisions or insurance risk assessment",
        "Generalising to other companies' customer support domains",
    ],
)

@dataclass
class DatasheetDistribution:
    access_mechanism: str         # "internal S3", "Hugging Face", "on request", etc.
    access_restrictions: str
    license: str
    license_url: str
    release_date: str
    version: str
    doi_or_citation: str

@dataclass
class DatasheetMaintenance:
    maintainer: str
    contact_email: str
    update_cadence: str
    known_issues: list[str]
    erratum_process: str
    deprecation_plan: str
    retain_older_versions: bool

distribution = DatasheetDistribution(
    access_mechanism="Internal S3 bucket — request via JIRA ticket AI-DATA-ACCESS",
    access_restrictions="AcmeCorp employees only; NDA required for contractors",
    license="Proprietary — AcmeCorp internal use only",
    license_url="https://internal.acmecorp.example/data-license",
    release_date="2024-11-15",
    version="1.2.0",
    doi_or_citation="Internal ref: DS-2024-CUST-SUPPORT-42K",
)

maintenance = DatasheetMaintenance(
    maintainer="AcmeCorp DataOps Team",
    contact_email="ai-data@acmecorp.example",
    update_cadence="Quarterly refresh with new tickets; version bump each refresh",
    known_issues=[
        "Slight over-representation of billing dispute tickets (30% vs est. 22% real distribution)",
        "Resolution_time field missing for 18% of records",
    ],
    erratum_process="File JIRA issue under project AI-DATA; fix in next quarterly refresh",
    deprecation_plan="Deprecate 18 months after release; archived for 36 months total",
    retain_older_versions=True,
)

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class DatasheetForDataset:
    """Complete datasheet following Gebru et al. (2021)."""
    dataset_name: str
    dataset_version: str
    motivation: DatasheetMotivation
    composition: DatasheetComposition
    collection: DatasheetCollection
    preprocessing: DatasheetPreprocessing
    uses: DatasheetUses
    distribution: DatasheetDistribution
    maintenance: DatasheetMaintenance

ACMECORP_SUPPORT_DATASHEET = DatasheetForDataset(
    dataset_name="AcmeCorp Customer Support Tickets 2023-2024",
    dataset_version="1.2.0",
    motivation=motivation,
    composition=composition,
    collection=collection,
    preprocessing=preprocessing,
    uses=uses,
    distribution=distribution,
    maintenance=maintenance,
)
A dataset used to train/evaluate models
ML practitioners, auditors, downstream dataset users
Collection method, composition, consent, permitted uses
New model version, evaluation, or incident
New data batch, known issue discovery, distribution change
EU AI Act Art. 11-13, NIST AI RMF
GDPR Art. 5 (purpose limitation), EU AI Act Art. 10 (data governance)
Yes, for high-risk AI (EU AI Act)
Not yet mandated but increasingly expected by GPAI obligations
describing consent mechanisms only in vague terms like "data was collected appropriately" without specifying the legal basis or mechanism.
not disclosing that PII was present before de-identification — relevant to auditors assessing re-identification risk.
listing "NLP tasks" instead of specific tasks the dataset was validated for, which leads to inappropriate out-of-distribution use.
without an explicit prohibition list, downstream users have no clear signal about what to avoid.
reporting counts at initial creation but not updating as the dataset is filtered, cleaned, or augmented in subsequent versions.
datasheets that have no stated maintenance contact or erratum process become stale and misleading.

Datasheets for Datasets provide a structured, question-driven framework for documenting the motivation, composition, collection process, preprocessing steps, intended uses, distribution terms, and maintenance plan for ML datasets. They surface the ethical and legal considerations that are often invisible in dataset release notes: how was consent obtained, what PII was present and how was it handled, which uses are explicitly prohibited, and who is responsible for errors. For LLM systems, training data quality and provenance are foundational to safety — a model is only as trustworthy as the data it learned from, and a datasheet is how you prove that trust is warranted.

A datasheet is not a defense of the dataset — it is an honest accounting of it. Disclosing a known data limitation or bias is not an admission of failure; it is the information a downstream practitioner needs to decide whether the dataset is appropriate for their use case.
